response:
To find the **characteristic polynomial** of a $ 3 \times 3 $ matrix $ A $, we use the formula:

$$
\text{Char}(A) = \det(A - \lambda I)
$$

Where $ I $ is the identity matrix of the same size as $ A $, and $ \lambda $ is a scalar variable.

---

### Step 1: Write down the matrix

$$
A = \begin{pmatrix}
\frac{189}{16} & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\
\frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} & -\frac{41}{16} \\
\frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 2: Subtract $ \lambda I $

$$
A - \lambda I = 
\begin{pmatrix}
\frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\
\frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\
\frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda
\end{pmatrix}
$$

---

### Step 3: Compute the determinant

We compute:

$$
\det(A - \lambda I) = \begin{vmatrix}
\frac{189}{16} - \lambda & -\frac{27}{4} & \frac{83}{8} \\
\frac{39}{8} & -\frac{43}{8} - \lambda & -\frac{41}{16} \\
\frac{21}{16} & \frac{213}{16} & \frac{35}{8} - \lambda
\end{vmatrix}
$$

$$
a = \frac{189}{16} - \lambda, \quad b = -\frac{27}{4}, \quad c = \frac{83
